 {"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-h2s","title":"[EPIC] Parallel Fetching with Graceful Degradation","description":"## Overview

Transform the usage fetching system to run provider queries in parallel and gracefully handle partial failures, showing available results even when some providers fail.

## Strategic Rationale

This is the #2 priority improvement because:

1. **Immediate performance improvement**: Going from sequential to parallel fetching provides 3-5x speedup for multi-provider queries. Every `--provider all` user benefits immediately.

2. **Reliability transformation**: Current behavior - if one provider fails, users might get nothing or confusing errors. New behavior - always show what's available with clear warnings for failures.

3. **Already have infrastructure**: The codebase uses tokio async. Implementation is straightforward with futures::join_all.

4. **User expectation alignment**: Modern CLI tools should be fast and resilient. Sequential fetching with all-or-nothing failure modes feels dated.

## Current vs. Desired Behavior

### Before (Sequential, Fragile)
```
$ caut usage --provider all
# Takes 8+ seconds (sequential fetches)
# If one provider times out, entire command may fail or hang
```

### After (Parallel, Resilient)
```
$ caut usage --provider all
# Takes 2-3 seconds (parallel fetches)
# Shows partial results with warnings

╭─ Claude (oauth) ──────────────────╮
│ Session: 45% ████████░░░░ 2h reset│
╰───────────────────────────────────╯

╭─ Codex (cli) ─────────────────────╮
│ Account: user@example.com         │
╰───────────────────────────────────╯

⚠ Gemini: Request timed out after 5s
⚠ Cursor: Authentication required (run: cursor auth login)

Completed in 2.3s (2 succeeded, 2 failed)
```

## Technical Approach

1. **Parallel execution**: Use `futures::future::join_all` to run all provider fetches concurrently
2. **Timeout per provider**: Each provider gets its own timeout (default 10s)
3. **Result aggregation**: Collect successes and failures separately
4. **Partial rendering**: Render successful results, then list failures with suggestions
5. **Summary footer**: Show timing and success/failure counts

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Multi-provider fetch is at least 3x faster than sequential
- [ ] Partial failures don't prevent showing successful results
- [ ] Failed providers show clear error messages with suggestions
- [ ] Summary shows timing and counts
- [ ] Works with --json output (failures in separate array)

## Architecture Notes

The key insight is that provider fetches are independent - there's no reason to wait for Claude before starting Codex. The current sequential approach is a holdover from simpler implementations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:50:13.289726838-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T02:50:13.289726838-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-h2s","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-d30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:51:23.173863706-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

-{"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-hws","title":"History: Implement history storage layer and queries","description":"## Task Overview

Implement the Rust storage layer for reading/writing usage history. Provides the data access API that other components will use.

## Parent EPIC
[EPIC] Historical Usage Tracking with Trend Visualization (coding_agent_usage_tracker-smv)

## Technical Requirements

### Storage Module API

```rust
// src/storage/history.rs

pub struct HistoryStore {
    conn: Connection,
}

impl HistoryStore {
    /// Create or open history database
    pub fn open(path: &Path) -> Result<Self>;
    
    /// Record a usage snapshot
    pub fn record_snapshot(&self, snapshot: &UsageSnapshot, provider: &Provider) -> Result<i64>;
    
    /// Get snapshots for a provider within time range
    pub fn get_snapshots(
        &self,
        provider: &Provider,
        from: DateTime<Utc>,
        to: DateTime<Utc>,
    ) -> Result<Vec<StoredSnapshot>>;
    
    /// Get latest snapshot for each provider
    pub fn get_latest_all(&self) -> Result<HashMap<Provider, StoredSnapshot>>;
    
    /// Get usage velocity (% change per hour) over recent window
    pub fn get_velocity(
        &self,
        provider: &Provider,
        window: Duration,
    ) -> Result<Option<f64>>;
    
    /// Get aggregated stats for time period
    pub fn get_stats(
        &self,
        provider: &Provider,
        period: StatsPeriod,
    ) -> Result<UsageStats>;
    
    /// Cleanup old snapshots
    pub fn cleanup(&self, retention_days: i32) -> Result<usize>;
}

#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct StoredSnapshot {
    pub id: i64,
    pub provider: Provider,
    pub fetched_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub source: String,
    pub primary_used_pct: Option<f64>,
    pub secondary_used_pct: Option<f64>,
    pub tertiary_used_pct: Option<f64>,
    pub cost_today_usd: Option<f64>,
    pub cost_mtd_usd: Option<f64>,
    pub credits_remaining: Option<f64>,
}

pub struct UsageStats {
    pub average_primary_pct: f64,
    pub max_primary_pct: f64,
    pub min_primary_pct: f64,
    pub total_cost: f64,
    pub sample_count: usize,
}

pub enum StatsPeriod {
    Today,
    Yesterday,
    Last7Days,
    Last30Days,
    ThisMonth,
    LastMonth,
    Custom { from: DateTime<Utc>, to: DateTime<Utc> },
}
```

### Query Optimization

- Use prepared statements for repeated queries
- Proper parameterization (no SQL injection)
- Efficient time range queries using indexes
- Limit result sets for memory efficiency

```rust
impl HistoryStore {
    fn prepare_statements(&self) -> Result<PreparedStatements> {
        Ok(PreparedStatements {
            insert_snapshot: self.conn.prepare(
                "INSERT INTO usage_snapshots (...) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ...)"
            )?,
            get_by_time_range: self.conn.prepare(
                "SELECT * FROM usage_snapshots 
                 WHERE provider = ? AND fetched_at BETWEEN ? AND ?
                 ORDER BY fetched_at DESC
                 LIMIT ?"
            )?,
            // ...
        })
    }
}
```

### Thread Safety

- Database connection per-thread or use connection pool
- Consider r2d2 for connection pooling if needed
- Ensure atomic writes with transactions where needed

## Integration Points

- Called by fetch pipeline to record snapshots
- Called by history command to retrieve data
- Called by prediction logic for velocity calculation
- Called by TUI dashboard for sparklines

## Deliverables

- [ ] `src/storage/history.rs` module
- [ ] Public API as specified above
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests
- [ ] Integration tests with real database
- [ ] Error handling with proper error types

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All API methods implemented and tested
- [ ] Queries return correct results
- [ ] Performance: <10ms for typical queries
- [ ] Proper error handling (no panics)
- [ ] Thread-safe operation","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T13:59:07.556680955-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T13:59:07.556680955-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-hws","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-i3v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T14:21:40.518996467-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-i3v","title":"History: Design usage history schema and migrations","description":"## Task Overview

Design and implement the SQLite schema for storing usage history snapshots. This is the data foundation for all historical features.

## Parent EPIC
[EPIC] Historical Usage Tracking with Trend Visualization (coding_agent_usage_tracker-smv)

## Technical Requirements

### Schema Design

```sql
-- Core snapshots table
CREATE TABLE usage_snapshots (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    provider TEXT NOT NULL,
    fetched_at TEXT NOT NULL,  -- ISO8601 timestamp
    source TEXT NOT NULL,       -- fetch source (oauth, cli, web, etc.)
    
    -- Primary rate window
    primary_used_pct REAL,
    primary_window_minutes INTEGER,
    primary_resets_at TEXT,
    
    -- Secondary rate window  
    secondary_used_pct REAL,
    secondary_window_minutes INTEGER,
    secondary_resets_at TEXT,
    
    -- Tertiary rate window (if applicable)
    tertiary_used_pct REAL,
    tertiary_window_minutes INTEGER,
    tertiary_resets_at TEXT,
    
    -- Cost data
    cost_today_usd REAL,
    cost_mtd_usd REAL,
    
    -- Credits (for providers like Codex)
    credits_remaining REAL,
    
    -- Identity info (denormalized for query simplicity)
    account_email TEXT,
    account_org TEXT,
    
    -- Metadata
    fetch_duration_ms INTEGER,
    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- Indexes for common queries
CREATE INDEX idx_snapshots_provider_time ON usage_snapshots(provider, fetched_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_snapshots_fetched_at ON usage_snapshots(fetched_at DESC);

-- Schema version tracking
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (
    version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### Migration Strategy

1. Check current schema version
2. Apply migrations sequentially
3. Handle existing databases gracefully
4. Rollback capability for safety

```rust
const MIGRATIONS: &[&str] = &[
    // v1: Initial schema
    include_str!("../migrations/001_usage_snapshots.sql"),
    // v2: Add cost fields
    include_str!("../migrations/002_add_cost_fields.sql"),
    // ...
];

fn run_migrations(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {
    let current_version = get_schema_version(conn)?;
    for (idx, migration) in MIGRATIONS.iter().enumerate() {
        let version = idx + 1;
        if version > current_version {
            conn.execute_batch(migration)?;
            set_schema_version(conn, version)?;
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

## Data Retention

- Default: 90 days of history
- Configurable via config file
- Auto-cleanup on startup and periodically
- Vacuum after cleanup for space reclamation

```rust
fn cleanup_old_snapshots(conn: &Connection, retention_days: i32) -> Result<usize> {
    let cutoff = Utc::now() - Duration::days(retention_days as i64);
    let deleted = conn.execute(
        "DELETE FROM usage_snapshots WHERE fetched_at < ?",
        [cutoff.to_rfc3339()],
    )?;
    if deleted > 0 {
        conn.execute_batch("VACUUM")?;
    }
    Ok(deleted)
}
```

## Deliverables

- [ ] Schema SQL file(s) in migrations/ directory
- [ ] Migration runner in storage module
- [ ] Schema version tracking
- [ ] Data retention/cleanup logic
- [ ] Unit tests for migrations
- [ ] Documentation of schema

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Fresh database creates schema correctly
- [ ] Existing databases migrate without data loss
- [ ] Indexes created for performance
- [ ] Cleanup removes old data correctly
- [ ] Schema documented in code comments","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T13:59:04.924498473-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T13:59:04.924498473-05:00"}

 {"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-i9x","title":"TUI: Design and implement core dashboard layout with ratatui","description":"## Summary
Design and implement the core TUI layout using ratatui with responsive panels.

## Background
The current watch mode is text-based. A proper TUI dashboard provides:
1. At-a-glance status for all providers
2. Real-time updating without screen flicker
3. Keyboard navigation
4. Responsive layout that adapts to terminal size

## Technical Design

### TUI Framework Selection
Using `ratatui` (formerly tui-rs) because:
- Actively maintained
- Good cross-platform support
- Flexible widget system
- Works with crossterm backend

### Dashboard Layout
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  caut dashboard                                        [?] Help  [q] Quit│
├────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Claude                            │  Codex                              │
│  ████████░░░░░░░░░░░░ 42%         │  ██████████████░░░░░░ 71%          │
│  Primary: 42% (resets 2h 15m)     │  Primary: 71% (resets 45m)         │
│  Secondary: 28% (resets 6d)       │                                     │
│  ✓ All systems operational        │  ⚠ Minor service disruption        │
│  Auth: Valid (expires 6h)         │  Credits: $47.50 remaining          │
├────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────┤
│  OpenRouter                        │  Cursor                             │
│  ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 18%         │  ██████████████████░░ 89%          │
│  Fast: 18%  Slow: 5%              │  Primary: 89% (resets 3h)           │
│  ✓ All systems operational        │  ⚠ Approaching limit                │
├────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────┤
│  History (last 24h)                          │ Budget Status             │
│  ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█▇▆▅▄▃▂▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█▇▆         │ Daily: $4.50/$10 ████░░   │
│  Claude ────  Codex ····           │ Weekly: $28/$50  █████░   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
│ Last updated: 2 seconds ago                    Press 'r' to refresh now  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Core Layout Implementation
```rust
use ratatui::{
    layout::{Constraint, Direction, Layout, Rect},
    widgets::{Block, Borders, Paragraph},
    Frame,
};

pub struct Dashboard {
    providers: Vec<ProviderPanel>,
    history_panel: HistoryPanel,
    budget_panel: BudgetPanel,
    selected_panel: usize,
}

impl Dashboard {
    pub fn render(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        let chunks = Layout::default()
            .direction(Direction::Vertical)
            .constraints([
                Constraint::Length(1),      // Header
                Constraint::Min(10),        // Main content
                Constraint::Length(3),      // History/Budget
                Constraint::Length(1),      // Footer
            ])
            .split(frame.size());
        
        self.render_header(frame, chunks[0]);
        self.render_providers(frame, chunks[1]);
        self.render_bottom_panels(frame, chunks[2]);
        self.render_footer(frame, chunks[3]);
    }
    
    fn render_providers(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect) {
        // Responsive grid layout based on terminal width
        let cols = if area.width >= 120 { 4 }
                  else if area.width >= 80 { 2 }
                  else { 1 };
        
        let provider_chunks = self.create_grid(area, cols, self.providers.len());
        
        for (i, (panel, chunk)) in self.providers.iter()
            .zip(provider_chunks.iter())
            .enumerate() 
        {
            let selected = i == self.selected_panel;
            panel.render(frame, *chunk, selected);
        }
    }
}
```

### Responsive Behavior
```rust
impl Dashboard {
    fn layout_for_width(&self, width: u16) -> LayoutMode {
        match width {
            0..=60 => LayoutMode::SingleColumn,
            61..=100 => LayoutMode::TwoColumn,
            101..=140 => LayoutMode::ThreeColumn,
            _ => LayoutMode::FourColumn,
        }
    }
    
    fn handle_resize(&mut self, width: u16, height: u16) {
        self.layout_mode = self.layout_for_width(width);
        // Recalculate panel sizes
        // Hide optional panels if height too small
    }
}
```

## Dependencies
```toml
# Cargo.toml
[dependencies]
ratatui = "0.26"
crossterm = "0.27"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dashboard renders correctly
- [ ] Layout adapts to terminal size
- [ ] No flicker on updates
- [ ] Header and footer display correctly
- [ ] Provider panels arranged in grid
- [ ] Bottom panels (history/budget) render
- [ ] Smooth resize handling

## Implementation Notes
- Use double-buffering to prevent flicker
- Render at 1 FPS to reduce CPU usage (increase on updates)
- Consider terminal color scheme detection
- Handle very small terminals gracefully (minimum size warning)

## Dependencies
- None (foundational for TUI)
- Used by all other TUI tasks

## Testing
- Unit tests for layout calculations
- Manual testing on various terminal sizes
- Test with tmux, screen, native terminals
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T14:15:28.638318133-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T14:15:28.638318133-05:00"}
 {"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-isd","title":"[EPIC] API Server Mode for Local Integrations","description":"## Overview

Run caut as a local HTTP server that other tools can query for usage data. This enables an ecosystem of integrations - custom dashboards, Raycast extensions, automation scripts, and other development tools can all consume caut data.

## Strategic Importance (Why P2)

- **Enables ecosystem**: Power users build custom integrations
- **Clean architecture**: caut becomes a "usage data service"
- **Future-proof**: Opens doors for community-built tools
- **Power user feature**: Narrower audience but high value for them

## User Value Proposition

```bash
$ caut serve --port 8420
caut API server running on http://localhost:8420

Endpoints:
  GET /usage           - All providers
  GET /usage/:provider - Single provider  
  GET /history         - Historical data
  GET /health          - Server health check

Press Ctrl+C to stop.
```

```bash
# Query from any tool
$ curl localhost:8420/usage | jq .claude.primary.used_percent
65.5

$ curl localhost:8420/usage/claude
{
  "provider": "claude",
  "primary": {
    "used_percent": 65.5,
    "resets_in_minutes": 145
  },
  "secondary": {
    "used_percent": 42.0,
    "resets_in_minutes": 8640
  },
  "cost_today": 12.34,
  "status": "operational",
  "updated_at": "2026-01-18T12:34:56Z"
}
```

### Use Cases

1. **Custom menubar apps**: macOS/Windows status bar showing usage
2. **Raycast/Alfred extensions**: Quick usage lookup
3. **Grafana dashboards**: Long-term usage visualization
4. **CI/CD pipelines**: Fail builds if usage too high
5. **Slack bots**: Team usage notifications
6. **Integration with other dev tools**: IDE extensions, etc.

## Technical Approach

### HTTP Server with Axum

```rust
use axum::{Router, routing::get, Json};

async fn serve(port: u16) -> Result<()> {
    let app = Router::new()
        .route("/health", get(handle_health))
        .route("/usage", get(handle_all_usage))
        .route("/usage/:provider", get(handle_provider_usage))
        .route("/history", get(handle_history))
        .route("/history/:provider", get(handle_provider_history))
        .route("/budgets", get(handle_budgets))
        .layer(CorsLayer::permissive());  // Local only, permissive OK
    
    let addr = SocketAddr::from(([127, 0, 0, 1], port));
    println!("caut API server running on http://{}", addr);
    
    axum::Server::bind(&addr)
        .serve(app.into_make_service())
        .await
}
```

### Endpoint Handlers

```rust
async fn handle_all_usage(State(state): State<AppState>) -> Json<AllUsageResponse> {
    // Reuse existing fetch logic, same as `caut usage --json`
    let snapshots = fetch_all_providers(&state.config).await;
    Json(AllUsageResponse::from(snapshots))
}

async fn handle_provider_usage(
    State(state): State<AppState>,
    Path(provider): Path<String>,
) -> Result<Json<ProviderUsageResponse>, StatusCode> {
    let provider = Provider::from_str(&provider)
        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;
    
    let snapshot = fetch_provider(&state.config, &provider).await
        .map_err(|_| StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)?;
    
    Ok(Json(ProviderUsageResponse::from(snapshot)))
}
```

### Caching Layer

Avoid hammering providers on every HTTP request:

```rust
async fn handle_all_usage(State(state): State<AppState>) -> Json<AllUsageResponse> {
    // Check cache first
    if let Some(cached) = state.cache.get("all_usage").await {
        if cached.age() < Duration::from_secs(30) {
            return Json(cached.data);
        }
    }
    
    // Fetch fresh data
    let fresh = fetch_all_providers(&state.config).await;
    state.cache.set("all_usage", fresh.clone()).await;
    
    Json(fresh)
}
```

### Server-Sent Events for Real-Time

Optional: streaming updates for dashboards

```rust
async fn handle_stream() -> Sse<impl Stream<Item = Result<Event, Infallible>>> {
    let stream = IntervalStream::new(interval(Duration::from_secs(30)))
        .then(|_| async {
            let data = fetch_all_providers().await;
            Ok(Event::default().json_data(data).unwrap())
        });
    
    Sse::new(stream).keep_alive(KeepAlive::default())
}
```

## API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `/health` | GET | Server health check |
| `/usage` | GET | All providers usage |
| `/usage/:provider` | GET | Single provider usage |
| `/history` | GET | Historical data (query params for filtering) |
| `/history/:provider` | GET | Provider-specific history |
| `/budgets` | GET | Budget status |
| `/stream` | GET | SSE real-time updates |

## Configuration

```bash
$ caut serve --help
Run caut as an HTTP API server

Options:
  -p, --port <PORT>      Port to listen on [default: 8420]
  -b, --bind <ADDR>      Address to bind [default: 127.0.0.1]
  --cache-ttl <SECS>     Cache TTL in seconds [default: 30]
  --cors                 Enable CORS (default: enabled for localhost)
```

## Security Considerations

- **Localhost only by default**: Bind to 127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0
- **No auth needed**: Local access only, trust the user
- **CORS permissive**: Needed for browser-based tools
- **Warning on non-local bind**: Clearly warn if exposing to network

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Server starts and responds to health checks
- [ ] All usage endpoints return correct data
- [ ] History endpoint with time range filtering
- [ ] Caching prevents excessive provider calls
- [ ] SSE streaming works for real-time updates
- [ ] Clean shutdown on Ctrl+C
- [ ] Clear documentation of all endpoints

## Dependencies

- **Benefits from**: All core features (history, budgets, etc.)
- **New dependency**: axum (lightweight, async-first HTTP)
- **Optional**: tower-http for middleware

## Considerations

- Port conflicts: What if 8420 is in use?
- Daemonization: Should there be a `--daemon` mode?
- Systemd integration: Service file for always-on operation
- Log output: Structured logging for debugging","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T13:58:07.308109376-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T13:58:07.308109376-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-isd","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-v3f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T14:21:34.434014002-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-isd","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-s46","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:16:43.383207391-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-isd","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-7ng","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:16:43.431839109-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-isd","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-teh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:16:43.478643236-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-isd","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-7zk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:16:43.525639905-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-isd","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-hlt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:16:43.573339237-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_usage_tracker-j5d","title":"TUI: Add keyboard navigation and controls","description":"## Summary
Implement keyboard navigation and interaction for the TUI dashboard.

## Background
A good TUI needs keyboard controls for:
1. Navigation between panels
2. Triggering actions (refresh, quit)
3. Expanding/collapsing details
4. Help overlay
5. Accessibility

## Technical Design

### Event Loop
```rust
use crossterm::event::{self, Event, KeyCode, KeyEvent, KeyModifiers};

pub struct DashboardApp {
    dashboard: Dashboard,
    running: bool,
    show_help: bool,
    selected_panel: usize,
}

impl DashboardApp {
    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        let mut terminal = self.setup_terminal()?;
        
        let tick_rate = Duration::from_millis(100);
        let mut last_tick = Instant::now();
        
        while self.running {
            terminal.draw(|f| self.render(f))?;
            
            // Handle events with timeout
            let timeout = tick_rate
                .checked_sub(last_tick.elapsed())
                .unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO);
            
            if event::poll(timeout)? {
                if let Event::Key(key) = event::read()? {
                    self.handle_key(key).await?;
                }
            }
            
            if last_tick.elapsed() >= tick_rate {
                self.tick().await?;
                last_tick = Instant::now();
            }
        }
        
        self.restore_terminal(&mut terminal)?;
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Key Handling
```rust
impl DashboardApp {
    async fn handle_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> Result<()> {
        // Help overlay takes precedence
        if self.show_help {
            if matches!(key.code, KeyCode::Esc | KeyCode::Char('?')) {
                self.show_help = false;
            }
            return Ok(());
        }
        
        match key.code {
            // Navigation
            KeyCode::Left | KeyCode::Char('h') => self.select_prev_panel(),
            KeyCode::Right | KeyCode::Char('l') => self.select_next_panel(),
            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => self.select_up_panel(),
            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => self.select_down_panel(),
            KeyCode::Tab => self.select_next_panel(),
            KeyCode::BackTab => self.select_prev_panel(),
            
            // Actions
            KeyCode::Char('r') => self.refresh_now().await?,
            KeyCode::Char('R') => self.refresh_all().await?,
            KeyCode::Enter => self.toggle_panel_detail(),
            KeyCode::Char(' ') => self.toggle_panel_detail(),
            
            // Time range
            KeyCode::Char('1') => self.set_history_range(Duration::hours(1)),
            KeyCode::Char('2') => self.set_history_range(Duration::hours(24)),
            KeyCode::Char('3') => self.set_history_range(Duration::days(7)),
            
            // Help and quit
            KeyCode::Char('?') => self.show_help = true,
            KeyCode::Char('q') => self.running = false,
            KeyCode::Esc => {
                if self.detail_expanded {
                    self.detail_expanded = false;
                } else {
                    self.running = false;
                }
            }
            
            // Ctrl+C to quit
            KeyCode::Char('c') if key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL) => {
                self.running = false;
            }
            
            _ => {}
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Panel Selection
```rust
impl DashboardApp {
    fn select_next_panel(&mut self) {
        let count = self.dashboard.providers.len();
        self.selected_panel = (self.selected_panel + 1) % count;
    }
    
    fn select_prev_panel(&mut self) {
        let count = self.dashboard.providers.len();
        self.selected_panel = self.selected_panel.checked_sub(1).unwrap_or(count - 1);
    }
    
    fn select_up_panel(&mut self) {
        let cols = self.dashboard.columns();
        if self.selected_panel >= cols {
            self.selected_panel -= cols;
        }
    }
    
    fn select_down_panel(&mut self) {
        let cols = self.dashboard.columns();
        let count = self.dashboard.providers.len();
        let new_idx = self.selected_panel + cols;
        if new_idx < count {
            self.selected_panel = new_idx;
        }
    }
}
```

### Help Overlay
```rust
impl DashboardApp {
    fn render_help(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        let help_text = vec![
            "",
            "  Navigation",
            "  ─────────────────────",
            "  h/←      Previous panel",
            "  l/→      Next panel",
            "  k/↑      Panel above",
            "  j/↓      Panel below",
            "  Tab      Next panel",
            "",
            "  Actions",
            "  ─────────────────────",
            "  r        Refresh selected",
            "  R        Refresh all",
            "  Enter    Toggle details",
            "",
            "  History Range",
            "  ─────────────────────",
            "  1        Last hour",
            "  2        Last 24 hours",
            "  3        Last 7 days",
            "",
            "  General",
            "  ─────────────────────",
            "  ?        Show/hide help",
            "  q/Esc    Quit",
            "",
        ];
        
        let block = Block::default()
            .title(" Help ")
            .borders(Borders::ALL)
            .border_style(Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan));
        
        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(help_text.join("\n"))
            .block(block)
            .alignment(Alignment::Left);
        
        // Center the help overlay
        let area = self.centered_rect(50, 80, frame.size());
        
        // Clear the background
        frame.render_widget(Clear, area);
        frame.render_widget(paragraph, area);
    }
    
    fn centered_rect(&self, percent_x: u16, percent_y: u16, r: Rect) -> Rect {
        let popup_layout = Layout::default()
            .direction(Direction::Vertical)
            .constraints([
                Constraint::Percentage((100 - percent_y) / 2),
                Constraint::Percentage(percent_y),
                Constraint::Percentage((100 - percent_y) / 2),
            ])
            .split(r);
        
        Layout::default()
            .direction(Direction::Horizontal)
            .constraints([
                Constraint::Percentage((100 - percent_x) / 2),
                Constraint::Percentage(percent_x),
                Constraint::Percentage((100 - percent_x) / 2),
            ])
            .split(popup_layout[1])[1]
    }
}
```

## Key Bindings Summary
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| h/← | Previous panel |
| l/→ | Next panel |
| k/↑ | Panel above |
| j/↓ | Panel below |
| Tab | Next panel |
| r | Refresh selected |
| R | Refresh all |
| Enter | Toggle details |
| 1/2/3 | History range |
| ? | Show help |
| q/Esc | Quit |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Arrow key navigation works
- [ ] Vi-style hjkl navigation works
- [ ] Tab cycles through panels
- [ ] Selected panel visually highlighted
- [ ] r refreshes selected provider
- [ ] R refreshes all providers
- [ ] Help overlay displays and dismisses
- [ ] q and Esc quit properly
- [ ] Ctrl+C quits properly
- [ ] History range keys work

## Accessibility
- All actions have keyboard shortcuts
- Selected item clearly visible
- Help always accessible
- No mouse required

## Dependencies
- Requires core TUI layout (sibling task)
- Requires provider panels (sibling task)
